summary: Excellent soundboard capture of a magnificent 1978 Capitol Theatre performance, praised for crystalline highs and vibrant playing.
review: The recording is lauded as one of the cleanest, full‑spectrum soundboard tapes in the Archive, with crystal‑clear highs and “A‑bomb” bass. Reviewers also describe the show itself as a terrific, magnificent performance that transports listeners back to the Capitol Theatre in 1978.

ai_rating.rationale: All five reviews are overwhelmingly positive (4‑5 stars) and specifically praise both the musical performance and the superb soundboard recording, indicating an exceptional show with excellent technical quality.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Reviewers highlight crystalline highs, deep bass, full‑spectrum clarity, and describe it as one of the finest sound‑spectrum recordings on the Archive.

show_quality.setlist_flow: No criticisms noted; reviewers emphasize a consistently strong performance that delivered a wonderful listening experience.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Multiple reviewers note Jerry's guitar was “alive and cooking” with perfect tone.
